hydrogel_name: LDI-cross-linked gelatin-based hydrogel thin film on amino-functional CVD polymer
hydrogel_format: thin surface-anchored hydrogel film on QCM-D sensor
monomers: ["gelatin from porcine skin (type A; gel strength ~175 bloom)"]
synthesis_method: CVD polymerization of amine-functional polymer support, spin-coating of 1 wt % gelatin solution, immersion in 10 mM LDI in DMF for 60 min under argon, then washing at 37 C.
preparation_solvent: aqueous gelatin solution; LDI cross-linking solution
preparation_temp_C: {"value":55,"unit":"°C","raw_text":"Gelatin (1 wt %) was dissolved in water under magnetic stirring at 55 °C"}
gelation_time: {"value":60,"unit":"min","raw_text":"immersing the substrate for 60 min in an LDI solution"}
post_treatment: washing after cross-linking
functional_groups: ["free amine groups","urea bonds","amine-functional CVD polymer anchoring groups"]
young_modulus: Reduced elastic modulus Er = 27 +/- 3 to 174 +/- 51 kPa at 25 C and 18 +/- 2 to 106 +/- 19 kPa at 37 C, depending on indentation force.
degradation_or_stability: Cross-linked gelatin-based hydrogel film remained on the substrate after washing; exposure to 10 M urea containing 0.1% SDS at pH 3.4 caused no film thickness decrease.
experiment_mode: flow-through QCM-D adsorption on coated quartz crystal followed by buffer rinse
contact_time: until stable frequency and dissipation baseline, followed by protein adsorption and buffer rinse
detection_method: QCM-D with Voigt viscoelastic model; ToF-SIMS/PCA after QCM-D for adsorbate characterization
outcome_label: HSA adsorption on gelatin hydrogel thin film
mechanism_tags: ["reduced adsorption","passivating gelatin layer","less densely packed protein layer","minor protein interaction with gelatin hydrogel"]
article_title: Interaction of Human Plasma Proteins with Thin Gelatin-Based Hydrogel Films: A QCM-D and ToF-SIMS Study
